label: Daily maintenance of the physical and relational substrate — clean room, laundry, dad
why: The maintenance tier is where most people's lives quietly decay while they're busy with the Big Important Work. Clean room daily, laundry weekly, something for dad daily — small enough that they don't compete with partnership-level work for attention, declared so that when a week goes by and three of them are broken it shows up as a visible pattern instead of a vague 'I feel off' feeling.

operational_shape.clean_room_today: Daily. Small action, compounds into a livable workspace. The anchor is the touch, not the depth of the cleaning.
operational_shape.laundry_this_week: Weekly. Missed weeks compound into a real problem fast.
operational_shape.something_for_dad_today: Daily, relational. Could be a text, a call, an errand, a small act. The 'something' is deliberately loose — the anchor is the daily touch, not a specific format.

notes: Declared 2026-04-22 in Jake's scope riff. Comparator hints: daily_physical_maintenance (room, laundry) and daily_family_fidelity (dad — lives in its own regime, relational not physical, shouldn't be scored against laundry). Weights moderate (0.40–0.55). These are not funny. They're the substrate under everything else.
